name: Bagels
recipeInstructions:
1. Combine yeast and 1/2 cup warm water in a measuring cup, stirring with a fork until dissolved.
2. In a bowl, combine 2 cups flour, salt, and sugar.
3. Add the dissolved yeast and remaining 1/2 cup warm water to the flour mixture. Beat vigorously with a whisk or spoon for 2 minutes.
4. Gradually add enough of the remaining flour, about 1/4 cup at a time, until a soft dough forms. Turn out onto a floured surface.
5. Knead until smooth and elastic, adding as little flour as necessary (about 8-10 minutes). Don't skimp on kneading!
6. Place dough in a bowl, cover with plastic wrap, and let rise until doubled in size (45-60 minutes).
7. Punch down the dough and divide into 12 equal portions (about 3-4 ounces each). Weigh dough on a kitchen scale for accuracy.
8. Form each portion into smooth balls, gently pulling sides of dough down and tucking ends underneath. Place on a floured surface and let rest 10 minutes.
9. Using a finger or wooden spoon handle, punch a hole in the center of each bagel. Twirl or stretch each ball into a bagel shape.
10. Cover with a towel and let rest another 20-30 minutes before cooking.
11. Bring a large shallow pan of water to a boil with 1 tablespoon sugar and 1 tablespoon salt.
12. Gently drop 3 or 4 bagels at a time into the simmering water. Cook each side for 2 minutes, then remove with a slotted spoon and place on a towel to cool.
13. Brush each bagel with egg white wash (1 egg white mixed with 1 tablespoon water) over the sink. Optionally sprinkle with salt, poppy seeds, minced garlic or onion, sesame seeds, etc.
14. Place bagels on a greased baking sheet and bake at 375 degrees Fahrenheit for 30 minutes. For a crisper crust, bake for 10 minutes, then brush with egg wash, add toppings, and return to oven for an additional 20 minutes.
15. Remove from the baking sheet and cool on wire racks.
